rulings.Case Title: COHISE THOMAS et al VS. ROSENDA REYES et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SAN MATEO COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Change Venue And Mtn For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 1: DEFENDANT ROSENDA REYES' Motion For Change Of Venue: OFF CALENDAR order changing venue filed on July 3, 2014. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA JANECEK VS. MIKE TAM et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANTS MIKE TAM and PING MAY's MOTION TO STRIKE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is GRANTED as to the request for injunction to abate the condition (FAC paragraph 77; Plaintiff's Opposition 8:15-17) as Plaintiff has vacated the premises (FAC, paragraph 23). Motion is otherwise denied. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA JANECEK VS. MIKE TAM et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 2: DEFENDANTS MIKE TAM and PING MAY's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurer to the second cause of action is moot, dismissal filed on July 3, 2014; Demurer to the fourth and fifth causes of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege an express tern f the lease breached by alleged failure to repair. To the extent these cause of action allege breached of implied covenants, they are duplicative of other causes of action alleged in the complaint; Demurer to the sixth, seventh, 8th and 10th causes of action is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleged each element of each cause of action. Demurer to the 9th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend, duplicative. Van Zyl v. Spiegelberg (1969) 2 Cal. 3d 367; El Escorial Owners' Association v. DLC Plastering (2007) 154 Cal.App.4th 1337. (501/lks)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA JANECEK VS. MIKE TAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 2: PLAINTIFF REBECCA JANECEK's Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7: DENIED. Moving party failed to show that defendant's demurrer violated any provisions of CCP 128.7(b). Also, see Court's ruling on Defendant's demurer. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: REBECCA JANECEK VS. MIKE TAM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Mike Tam, Ping May'S Motion For Protective Order To Prohibit Deposition Of Ping May; Request For Sanctions; Proposed Order
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 2: Defendants Mike Tam and Ping May'S Motion For Protective Order To Prohibit Deposition Of Ping May; Request For Sanctions; Proposed Order: DENIED without prejudice. The only piece of competent evidence (two line note from Joseph Leung, MD) is insufficient to support defendant's claims regarding Defendant's inability to testify, lack of knowledge, or undue burden. Sanctions granted in the amount of $900. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: CAL-MURPHY, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. MG RESTAURANTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Mg Restaurants Inc'S Demurrer To The Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 3: Defendant Mg Restaurants Inc'S Demurrer To The Complaint: OFF CALENDAR Without Prejudice to Renewal. This action is stayed until case CGC-08-473750 and associated appeals are resolved. Defendant may file a renewed demurer at that time. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: EILEEN DUFFY et al VS. MURPHY INVESTMENTS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANT DAVID WASSERMAN's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OFF CALENDAR, heard on June 27, 2014. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: EILEEN DUFFY et al VS. MURPHY INVESTMENTS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANTS MURPHY INVESTMENTS and BART MURPHY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OFF CALENDAR, duplicative. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: EILEEN DUFFY et al VS. MURPHY INVESTMENTS et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 4: DEFENDANTS MURPHY INVESTMENTS and BART MURPHY's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT: OVERRULED with 5 days to answer. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges each element of each cause of action as to the moving defendant. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: DEMMIE B. ACOSTA VS. FIDELITY NATIONAL TITLE COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Order To Show Cause Re: Temporary Restraining Order And Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 5: Order To Show Cause Re: Temporary Restraining Order And Preliminary Injunction: DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: CENTRO CULTURAL SERVICO DE LAS AMERICAS VS. ROBERT LO VERSO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Continue Trial
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 6: PLAINTIFF CENTRO CULTURAL SERVICO DE LAS AMERICAS' Motion To Continue Trial: GRANTED. Trial is continued to August 4, 2013 at 9:30 AM in Dept. 501. If deposition does not proceed during the week of July 21, 2014 due to Plaintiff, Defendant may seek trial continuance by ex-parte motion. (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: CENTRO CULTURAL SERVICO DE LAS AMERICAS VS. ROBERT LO VERSO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Consolidate Other Pending Action
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 6: PLAINTIFF CENTRO CULTURAL SERVICO DE LAS AMERICAS' Motion To Consolidate Other Pending Action: DENIED, no issues remain unresolved in case 647956. Plaintiff failed to comply with CRC 3.350(a). (501/LKS)

rulings.Case Title: MELINDA HOM TRUSTEE OF THE JAMES LEE 2010 VS. FEI LA JIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, July 17, 2014, Line 7: DEFENDANT FEI JIA's DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. (501/LKS)
